controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.3830463

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday July 28, 2021 at 03:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bernard Quaritch on the Hazel M. Osborn Fund, 2012.

bioghist.head: Shackleton family
bioghist.p: The Shackleton family were Quaker educators and writers living in Ballitore, Ireland, where Abraham Shackleton I founded the Ballitore School in 1726. The family also owned a number of flour mills.

bioghist.head: Abraham Shackleton I (1697-1771)
bioghist.p: Abraham Shackleton was born in Yorkshire, England, to Sarah Brigg and Richard Shackleton. He founded the Ballitore School, a Quaker school in Ballitore, Ireland, in 1726, and was headmaster for 30 years. One of his pupils was Edmund Burke.

bioghist.head: Richard Shackleton (1726-1792)
bioghist.p: Richard Shackleton, son of Margaret Wilkinson and Abraham Shackleton I, was educated at the Ballitore School, where he befriended fellow student Edmund Burke. He graduated from Trinity College, Dublin, and succeed his father as headmaster of Ballitore School in 1756. He was also a poet. Shackleton married Elizabeth Fuller in 1749 and they had four children, Abraham II, Deborah, Margaret, and Henry. A year after his first wife's death in 1754, he married Elizabeth Carleton, with whom he had two children who survived to adulthood, Mary Shackleton Leadbeater and Sarah, known to the family as "Blind Aunt Sally."

bioghist.head: Abraham Shackleton II (1752-1818)
bioghist.p: Abraham Shackleton II, son of Elizabeth Fuller and Richard Shackleton, was educated at Ballitore School and later became a teacher there, before succeeding his father as headmaster in 1779. Shackleton became associated with a group of liberal Quakers in Ulster who were in open rebellion against Quaker discipline. In 1801 he was disowned by the Carlow Monthly Meeting, in a schism within the Society of Friends in Ireland that was known as the "Irish separation." As a pacifist, he refused to join rebel forces occupying Ballitore during the Irish Rebellion in 1798. Following the schism and the brief closure of the Ballitore School, Shackleton traveled, wrote essays and poetry, and managed the Ballitore Mill until his death. Shackleton's scholarly interests included the classics, botany, and astronomy. He married Lydia Mellor (1749-1829) in 1779, and they had nine children: Richard, Margaret (who died at age 9), Abraham III, Elizabeth "Betsy" (1783-1843), Ebenezer, George, Lydia, Mary, and Margaret Shackleton Grubb.

bioghist.head: Mary Shackleton Leadbeater (1758-1826)
bioghist.p: Mary Shackleton Leadbeater, daughter of Elizabeth Carleton and Richard Shackleton, was educated at the Ballitore School, where she was the only female student. In 1794, she published anonymously "Extracts and Original Anecdotes for the Improvement of Youth." She also published "Poems" in 1808; "Cottage Dialogues among the Irish Peasantry" in 1811; and "Memoirs and Letters of Richard and Elizabeth Shackleton" in 1822. Her "Annals of Ballitore" was printed posthumously in 1862 by Richard Davis Webb as "The Leadbeater Papers." Mary married William Leadbeater, a former student at the Ballitore School, in 1791, and they had three children. The Leadbeaters resided in Ballitore, where he was a farmer and landowner, and she ran the village post office.

bioghist.head: Sarah Shackleton (1760-1847)
bioghist.p: Sarah Shackleton, daughter of Elizabeth Carleton and Richard Shackleton, known as "Sally" or "Blind Aunt Sally," was an Acceptable Minister in the Society of Friends.

bioghist.head: George Shackleton (1785-1871)
bioghist.p: George Shackleton, son of Lydia and Abraham Shackleton II, married Hannah Fisher (1803-1873) in 1823, and they had 13 children who lived to adulthood, including Abraham III and Lydia.

bioghist.head: Abraham Shackleton III (1827-1912)
bioghist.p: Abraham Shackleton III married Anna Webb (1833-1888), and they had six children: Louis George, William Webb, Frederic, Marion, Alice Mary, and John Abraham. After her death, he married Anne Harvey Walpole.

bioghist.head: Lydia Shackleton II (1828-1914)
bioghist.p: Lydia Shackleton, a daughter of Hannah and George Shackleton, was botanist and artist.

bioghist.head: William Webb Shackleton (1867-1935)
bioghist.p: William Webb Shackleton was the eldest son of Anna Webb and Abraham Shackleton III. He was educated at the Quaker school in York and trained as a doctor. In 1896, he married his senior partner's daughter, Eileen Clarke (1879-1955). They had two children, Robert Patrick Webb and Eileen Margaret.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Shackleton Family Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, diaries, travel journals, commonplace books, poems, genealogical documents, and testimonials and memoirs of the Shackleton family, who were Quaker educators, writers, and mill owners in Ireland. The materials document the family's involvement in the Society of Friends, their personal lives and intellectual pursuits, and work at the Ballitore School, a Quaker school founded by Abraham Shackleton I in 1726. Included is extensive correspondence and writings of Richard Shackleton, his son Abraham Shackleton II and daughter Mary Leadbeater Shackleton, and physician William Webb Shackleton, a son of Abraham Shackleton III. Among the other correspondents were family members Elizabeth, Sarah, George, and Lydia Shackleton, and Quaker friends such as British author Joseph Gurney Bevan, British minister Samuel Bownas, American ministers Rebecca Jones and Job Scott, and Dublin physician John Rutty. Topics of letters include matters concerning the Quaker community, smallpox inoculation, and the Irish Rebellion of 1798. The collection dates from 1658 to 1944, with the bulk dating from approximately 1770 to 1884.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Shackleton Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1727-1944. II. Writings and Other Papers, 1678-1888. III. Printed Materials, 1765-1884.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence between Shackleton family members, with some correspondence of the Mellor, Leadbeater, Carleton, and Grubb families. Topics of letters include personal news, travel, matters concerning the Quaker community, smallpox inoculation, and the Irish Rebellion of 1798. Abraham Shackleton II often writes about events and students at the Ballitore School. Among the other correspondents were Quaker friends such as British author Joseph Gurney Bevan, British minister Samuel Bownas, American ministers Rebecca Jones and Job Scott, and Dublin physician John Rutty.

did.unittitle: Abraham, Eleanor. Letter to Lydia Shackleton
Buck, Andrew. Letter to Richard Shackleton, 1747 (photocopy)
Carleton, Deborah. Letter to Sarah (Sally) Shackleton
Carleton, Samuel. Letter to Sarah (Sally) Shackleton
[Grubb], Margaret Shackleton. Letter to Abraham Shackleton
Grubb, Margaret Shackleton. Letter (fragment) to Lydia Shackleton
Grubb, Sarah. Letter to Elizabeth Carleton Shackleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the death of Richard Shackleton.

did.unittitle: Jones, Rebecca. Letter to Sarah (Sally) Shackleton
Leadbeater, Deborah. Letter to Margaret Shackleton Grubb
Leadbeater, Elizabeth, and Mary S. Leadbeater. Letters to George Shackleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes several letters from Lydia Leadbeater.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the death of her grandfather Abraham Shackleton.

did.unittitle: LeFann, William. Letter to Elizabeth (Betsy) Shackleton
Mellor, Ebenezer and Margaret. Letters from family and friends

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the death of Matthew Mellor.

did.unittitle: Mellor, Samuel and Lydia. Letters from Quaker friends
Letter from Lydia Mellor to J. Richardson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include the Jacobite rising of 1745.

did.unittitle: Newenham, George. Letters to Abraham Shackleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include the Aurora Borealis, the construction of a hygrometer and balloons, and astronomer William Herschel. Newenham also offers instructions for using a solar telescope.

did.unittitle: Palmer, Sanford. Letter to Mary Shackleton Leadbeater

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Account of the Irish Rebellion of 1798 in Ballitore.

did.unittitle: Reynolds, Richard. Letter to Abraham Shackleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the death of Richard Shackleton.

did.unittitle: Rutty, John. Letter to Abraham Shackleton (1697-1771)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning the Society of Friends and discipline.

did.unittitle: Scott, Job. Letter to Elizabeth (Betsy) Shackleton
Shackleton, Abraham, 1697-1771. Copy of three letters to Margaret Shackleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several letters also contain messages from other family members.

did.unittitle: Letters to Elizabeth (Betsy) Shackleton and Margaret Shackleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to Betsy from her siblings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding inoculation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes London trip with his grandfather Abraham Shackleton.

did.unittitle: Letters to Richard Shackleton, and one letter from Richard Shackleton to Sarah (Sally) Shackleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comments on inoculation.

did.unittitle: Two unsigned letters to an unknown recipient

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Addressed "To the Author" and "Mr. Anonymous."

did.unittitle: Letters and testimonies regarding Richard Shackleton after his death in 1792
Shackleton, Abraham. Letter to Elizabeth Carleton Shackleton
Shackleton, Abraham. Letters to his children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics also include the Irish Rebellion.

did.unittitle: Shackleton, Elizabeth, and Sarah Dunleavy. Letter to Abraham Shackleton
Bevan, Joseph G. Letter to Abraham Shackleton, "In Character of R[ichard] Shackleton"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding a fire in Ballitore.

did.unittitle: Shackleton, Anne. Letters to Peggie Larsen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: References World War II.

did.unittitle: Shackleton, Deborah. Letter to Lydia Mellor [Shackleton]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discusses the deaths of Abraham Shackleton I, Aldoborough "Alby" Wrightson (1746-1771), and others.

did.unittitle: Shackleton, Elizabeth Carleton, and Mary S. Leadbeater. Letter to Sarah (Sally) Shackleton
Correspondence with Abraham and Lydia Shackleton
Letter to Mary Shackleton (including one dictated to Hannah Shackleton)
Letter to unknown recipient, possibly Sarah (Sally) Shackleton
Letters from Margaret Grubb and other Grubb family members
Letters from Mary, Elizabeth, and Lydia Leadbeater
Letters to Abraham Shackleton, with letter to an unknown friend upon her husband's death, and letter to Elizabeth Leadbeater
Letters to Sarah (Sally) Shackleton and Richard Shackleton
Letters from Thomas Abraham (uncle) and Robert Lecky
Shackleton, Lydia, 1828-1914. Letter to Hannah Fisher Shackleton
Shackleton, Margaret (Maggie). Letters to Elizabeth Shackleton Webb
Letters to Sarah (Sally) Shackleton, with one letter from Elizabeth Shackleton
Letters to Sarah (Sally) Shackleton, with five letters from Elizabeth Shackleton
Extract (copy) regarding matrimony from a letter to an unknown recipient in 1777

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Describes experience with physicians when she first started going blind.

did.unittitle: Letters from his father A[braham] S[hackleton], mother Anna Webb, and sisters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Topics include William Ewart Gladstone's Midloathian campaign and Irish politics. There is also a list of girls enrolled in Mount School, York, in 1883.

did.unittitle: Webb, Maria. Letters and photographs to Lucy Lamb

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Anna Webb Shackleton, Abraham Shackleton III, and an unnamed child.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains commonplace books, diaries, and poems by Mary Shackleton Leadbeater, Richard Shackleton, Lydia Shackleton, and others, as well as testimonials and memoirs recalling family members after their deaths. Included are the travel journals of Abraham Shackleton II from his journeys to Northern Ireland, Munster, and Dunmore. There are also family trees and genealogical research by Maria Webb, and manuscripts of magazines printed by the Ballitore School.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author or folder title.

did.unittitle: Centenary dinner ticket for George Shackleton, enclosed with letter from Edward Maguire
Carlow Monthly Meeting. Proposed altered draft and final draft of a testimony given forth by the Carlow Monthly Meeting respecting our dear deceased friend Richard Shackleton
Concerning Lydia S[hackleton] on her deathbed
Cork Gazette . Decease of Richard Shackleton, manuscript extract
Letters copied from the Swarthmoor papers by Maria Webb

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extracts of the letters were published in Webb's book Fells of Swarthmoor Hall .

did.unittitle: Pedigree of the Descendants of Fell of Swarthmoor Hall [family tree, photocopy of printed version]
LeFann, William."To Deborah [Leadbeater]," manuscript copied by Lydia Leadbeater
"Epithalamium Addressed to Abram. Shackleton, on his Marriage with Lydia Mellor"
"On a bag & purse," to Margaret Shackleton
"On the death of my beloved Brother: Addressed to R.S."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "M.L. to L.S." is written on the verso.

did.unittitle: "On the departure of Eben[ezer] from Ballitore"
Commonplace book, with additions by William H. Harvey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dialogue in the form of a play, with a list of characters played by Deborah Carleton, Margaret "Peggy" and Mary "Molly" Shackleton, and children.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of Frances Paxton Henshaw's account of her experience converting to Quakerism, with her letters to George II (1745) and George III (1774).

did.unittitle: Regarding Ebenezer Shackleton after his death at 92
Sarah Thompson and William Fadwen [?] marriage certificate
Proposed altered draft of a testimony respecting Richard Shackleton
Tramp to the North: A visit of AS & his daughters Betsy & Mary to their friends in the summer of 1808

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On a trip to Northern Ireland, Shackleton writes about architecture, topography, and vegetation, visiting the Giant's Ring, the Moravian settlement at Gracehill, and Dunluce Castle, and touring a damask factory.

did.unittitle: Eight weeks tour: Abrm Shackleton visits his friends in Munster October 1814
Pamphlets on education of the poor, food, and pauperism
Abraham Shackleton's Account of his father [Richard Shackleton] who died 8th month 22nd 1792 [notebook]
Ex Officio address to the Dublin Friends Institute [notebook]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes her recollection of the life of her father Abraham Shackleton.

did.unittitle: "Memoirs of Mary Leadbeater's character. Written by Elizth Shackleton. Copied for Thomas Fisher M.D. by his affect sister S.F." [notebook]
Shackleton, George. Extract from Lydia Shackleton's will

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries cover her personal life, interest in art, thoughts on religion and social issues, and books she was reading.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Verse addressed to his friend John Gough (1720-1791), master of the Friends' school in Dublin and Quaker historian, on Gough's 40th birthday.

did.unittitle: "On the Birth of My Grandson," for Lydia Shackleton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on the verso from Abraham Shackleton, dated 1800, reads, "Found in my dear fathers desk."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily copies of correspondence between Wrightson and Richard Shackleton.

did.unittitle: Society of Friends. Copy of certificate from York for Abraham Shackleton 7 Mo. 1721

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recommendation of Shackleton from the Society of Friends in York, addressed to the monthly meeting of Friends in Carlow.

did.unittitle: Testimonies concerning Lydia Mellor (1693-1754) and Matthew Mellor (died 1770)
"William Taylor's Expressions; Lydia Mellor: Some Account of Her" [notebook]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in two hands and bound in a paper cover of "Rules for Conducting the Ballitore Daily Schools; with the report for 1817."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains published works owned or authored by members of the Shackleton family and their friends. Most of the volumes contain inscriptions or bookplates.

did.unittitle: Behmen, Jacob [Bohme, Jakob]. Important truths relating to spiritual and practical Christianity

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With ownership inscription of "Experience Cooper Sept 1770" on front endpaper, and "Gift of Sar. Cooper to Abrm Shackleton" on front pastedown.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With bookplate of Abraham Shackleton, Ballitore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With book-prize labels presenting volume to Aldborough Wrightson, 1765.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With book-prize labels presenting volume to Aldborough Wrightson, 1765.

did.unittitle: Leadbeater, Mary. Memoirs and Letters of Richard and Elizabeth Shackleton
Patrick, Samuel. Clavis Homerica: Sive Lexicon Vocabulorum Omnium

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With bookplate and signature of Abraham Shackleton, Ballitore.

did.unittitle: Plutarch. Morals, vol. l, second edition, corrected and amended

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With bookplate of Abraham Shackleton, Ballitore. Also inscribed with initials JH and WH.

title.part: Publii Terentii comoediae sex quibus accedunt notae marginales John Minn-ellii

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writing in multiple hands on the endpapers, including inscription of Thomas Harrison (1772), William Marsden, John Marsden (1758), and R.E. (1781).

did.unittitle: Tuke, Henry. Biographical notices of the Society of Friends, Vol. 1: Life of George Fox

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed by Abraham Shackleton, 1835.

did.unittitle: Webb, Maria. The Penns and Peningtons of the Seventeenth Century

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed by Alice S. Shackleton, 1884.
